description: Constraint-type-agnostic anomaly injection experiment to address AUPRC information leakage
motivation: Original 18% injection deliberately triggers range/FD/logic CFD rules, creating information leakage. New 5% injection uses constraint-type-agnostic anomalies (random replacement, missing values, type mismatch) that do not specifically target any CFD constraint type.

unbiased.type_a: Random value replacement from same column (~1.7%)
unbiased.type_b: Missing value injection: NaN for numerical, empty string for categorical (~1.7%)
unbiased.type_c: Type mismatch: string 'TYPE_ERROR' injected into numerical columns (~1.7%)

biased_original.dependency_destroy: Swap MonthlyCharges between records (10%) - targets FD rules
biased_original.range_violation: Set values to extreme percentiles (5%) - targets range rules
biased_original.logic_violation: Set tenure to negative values (3%) - targets logic rules

preprocessing: All methods receive same preprocessed data: strings in numerical columns converted to NaN via pd.to_numeric(errors='coerce'), then imputed with median (numerical) or mode (categorical) from clean data.

comparison.note: Negative drop means the method performed BETTER on unbiased injection. LLM-CFD is expected to show the largest drop, confirming information leakage.

key_findings.finding_1: LLM-CFD AUPRC dropped from 0.5080 (biased 18%) to 0.0970 (unbiased 5%), a 80.9% drop. This is the largest drop among all methods, confirming information leakage.
key_findings.finding_2: LLM-CFD lift over random dropped from 2.98x to 1.91x, while EIF dropped from 1.25x to 1.06x. LLM-CFD had the largest lift drop (35.7%).
key_findings.finding_3: LLM-CFD relative advantage over EIF decreased from 2.38x to 1.81x; over COPOD from 2.85x to 1.96x.
key_findings.finding_4: Per-type analysis: LLM-CFD performs best on Type C (type mismatch, lift=2.54x) because imputed values can break consistency constraints. All methods struggle with Type B (missing values).
key_findings.finding_5: Under unbiased injection, all methods perform close to random (lift < 2x). LLM-CFD retains a small advantage (1.91x) over EIF (1.06x) and COPOD (0.98x), suggesting some genuine detection capability beyond information leakage.
